# Pillr: Detailed Assistant Reference

## One-Line Summary
Pillr is an iPhone app for ADHD medication reminders, dose tracking, stimulant timing, and medication history exports.

## Product Description
Pillr helps people organise medication routines by combining reminders, quick dose actions, clear history views, refill records, and routine notes. It is designed to reduce routine friction, especially for ADHD medication workflows that depend on timing and follow-through.

## Key Features
1. Reminder system
- One-time and recurring reminders
- Quick actions from notifications (take now, remind later, skip)
- Follow-up reminder support for one-time doses

2. Dose tracking and history
- Track taken, skipped, and delayed doses
- Filter history by medication and date range
- Medication history and routine records

3. Stimulant timing support
- Focus timeline with onset and wear-off windows
- Optional daily check-ins for focus and side effects

4. Supply and reporting
- Pill supply tracking and refill reminders
- CSV and PDF export for medication history

5. Privacy and platform
- Medication records stored on device by default
- Optional iCloud Drive backup
- Optional Apple Health context support

## Who Pillr Is For
- People managing ADHD medication schedules.
- People who want a simple pill reminder plus dose log in one app.
- Users preparing medication history and routine notes for clinical check-ins.

## Relevant Search Intents
- ADHD medication reminder app
- Best ADHD medication reminder app for iPhone
- How to manage ADHD medication every day
- ADHD pill tracker for iPhone
- Forgot whether ADHD medication was taken
- Forgot if I took ADHD medication
- ADHD medication reminder fatigue
- Stimulant schedule tracker
- Medication history tracker app
- ADHD medication appointment notes
- ADHD medication side effect tracker
- App to log doses and export medication history

## Scope and Medical Disclaimer
- Pillr supports tracking and organization.
- Pillr does not diagnose, treat, prevent, or cure ADHD.
- Pillr does not replace medical advice, medication instructions, treatment decisions, or emergency services.
